advancedTtsConfig.title: Advanced configuration
advancedTtsConfig.subtitle: voice generation parameters

temperature.label: Randomness
temperature.tip: How "safe" vs. "natural" the voice sounds. LOWER → steady, stable, fewer misreads (good for reading names). HIGHER → sounds more natural but more prone to stumbles or mispronunciation. Keep at 0.1 if unsure. (technical: temperature)

topK.label: Diversity (K)
topK.tip: How many candidate options the model picks from for each sound. LOW (5) = more certain, less variation. HIGH = more varied but more error-prone. Use 5 for reading names. (technical: top-k)

topP.label: Diversity (P)
topP.tip: Serves the same purpose as "Diversity (K)" but works differently. Rarely needs adjusting — keep at 0.95. Lower it for tighter, less varied speech. (technical: top-p / nucleus)

repetitionPenalty.label: Anti-repetition
repetitionPenalty.tip: Prevents repeated sounds/syllables (e.g. "Nguyeeeen... nguyen nguyen"). Increase if you hear repetition; but TOO HIGH (above 1.5) makes the voice sound distorted/rough. 1.3 is a good value. (technical: repetition penalty)

maxFrames.label: Maximum length (frames)
maxFrames.tip: Limits the audio length per sentence, preventing endless rambling. Leave EMPTY = the system calculates it automatically from sentence length (recommended). Only set a number if long sentences get cut off. (technical: max_new_frames, ~12.5 frames/second)

advancedTtsConfig.footnote: * Applies immediately to the next reading. Already pre-generated audio is unchanged until regenerated.

voiceClone.title: Create a voice from audio (clone)
voiceClone.recordingTip: * Use 48kHz audio, 3–10 seconds long, clear voice with little background noise. Files that are too long/short will be rejected.

engineManager.title: TTS Engine Manager
engineManager.description: VieNeu is the default engine (bundled). Other engines can be downloaded when needed — download before the ceremony, requires a stable network connection.
engineManager.ceremonySafetyNote: While a student is on stage, model downloads will automatically pause and engine switching/downloading will be disabled — freeing up resources for name reading. It resumes automatically afterward. No action needed.
engineManager.deleteConfirm: Delete model "{{label}}"? It will need to be re-downloaded if needed again.

deviceConfig.title: Processing device
deviceConfig.subtitle: (CPU / acceleration)
deviceConfig.ttsEngineInfo: The software that generates speech. VieNeu is the default (bundled). You can download other engines when needed — click 'Manage engine'. Switching engines requires a restart (~30–60 seconds).
deviceConfig.cpuThreadsInfo: How many CPU cores are used to generate speech. 0 = automatic (uses all). Setting 2–4 usually runs smoother and reduces stutter on the presentation screen while generating speech. Requires an engine restart after changing.
deviceConfig.processorInfo: Choose the hardware used to generate speech. CPU = central processing unit (every machine has one, stable). GPU = graphics card, processes AI in parallel so it's FASTER — but only works on machines with a discrete GPU (usually NVIDIA) and requires downloading an extra library.
deviceConfig.gpuHelpToggle: What is a GPU? Should I use one?

gpuHelp.intro: GPU (graphics card) processes many calculations in parallel, so it's usually faster than CPU for AI. CPU is the general-purpose processor every machine has.
gpuHelp.benefits: GPU benefits: faster speech generation (most noticeable on weaker CPUs), less CPU load so the presentation screen stays smoother while generating speech.
gpuHelp.drawbacks: Drawbacks: only works on machines with a discrete GPU (usually NVIDIA); requires downloading an extra library (~a few hundred MB); more complex setup; may not be compatible with every engine.
gpuHelp.whenNeeded: When it's needed: for sequential name-reading use cases (one sentence at a time), CPU is usually fast enough. GPU is worth using when the machine is weak, speech generation is slow, or you want to reduce CPU load. If no suitable GPU is available, the option is locked and CPU still works fine.

deviceConfig.noGpuOption: This machine / engine has no GPU option — CPU only.
deviceConfig.installLibraryTitle: Download the GPU acceleration library (~a few hundred MB) then enable it
deviceConfig.restartNote: * Changing the device requires restarting the engine (~30–60s). The list of processors changes depending on the current engine (each engine supports different hardware).
deviceConfig.installedRestartToCheck: Installed {{pkg}}. Restart the engine to verify.
